name: heatrapy

metadata.name: heatrapy
metadata.summary: Library for simulating 1D and 2D heat transfer processes

documentation: This package is a module for simulating dynamic 1D and 2D heat transfer processes by using the finite difference method. The packages is based on thermal objects. Two different approaches can be used: single thermal object and a system of thermal objects that can contact with each other. The heatrapypackage includes the incorporation of phase transitions.

modules.name: heatrapy.mats
documentation:
Mats.

This submodule access the properties of materials

modules.name: heatrapy
documentation:
Heatrapy.

author: Daniel Silva (djsilva@gmx.com)
current version: v2.0.11
url: https://github.com/djsilva99/heatrapy

This module allows to create thermal objects, establish thermal contact between
them, activate or deactivate the whole, or part, of the materials, and compute
the respective heat transfer processes, in 1D and 2D.

The module is based on four main classes:

SingleObject1D
--------------
This class computes the heat transfer processes involved in only one
unidimensional thermal object. It uses the class Object1D changing the state,
modifying the thermal properties and activate or deactivate the material.

SingleObject2D
--------------
This class computes the heat transfer processes involved in only one
two-dimensional thermal object. It uses the class Object2D changing the state,
modifying the thermal properties and activate or deactivate the material.

SystemObjects1D
---------------
This class creates a system of unidimensional thermal objects that can be in
contact to each other and computes the respective heat transfer processes. It
uses the class Object1D changing the state, modifying the thermal properties
and activate or deactivate the material.

SystemObjects2D
---------------
This class creates a system of two-dimensional thermal objects that can be in
contact to each other and computes the respective heat transfer processes. It
uses the class Object2D changing the state, modifying the thermal properties
and activate or deactivate the material.

modules.name: heatrapy.mats.calmatpro
documentation:
Contains the class calmatpro.

Used to access the properties of materials from the database.

classes.name: heatrapy.mats.calmatpro.CalMatPro

methods.name: heatrapy.mats.calmatpro.CalMatPro.__init__
documentation:
Thermal properties from materials folder.

`name_tadi` is the adiabatic temperature increase file and `name_tadd`
is the adiabatic temperature decrease file. `name_cp0` is the specific
heat file and `name_cpa` is the active specific heat file. `name_k0` is
the thermal conductivity file and `name_ka` is the active thermal
conductivity file. `name_rho0` is the density file and `name_rhoa` is
the active density file. `name_lheat0` and `name_lheata` are the latent
heat files when the material status is True or False, respectively.

methods.name: heatrapy.mats.calmatpro.CalMatPro.tadi
documentation:
Adiabatic temperature increase.

Gives the adiabatic temperature increase for a given temperature

methods.name: heatrapy.mats.calmatpro.CalMatPro.tadd
documentation:
Adiabatic temperature decrease.

Gives the adiabatic temperature decrease for a given temperature

methods.name: heatrapy.mats.calmatpro.CalMatPro.cpa
documentation:
Active specific heat.

Gives the active specific heat for a given temperature

methods.name: heatrapy.mats.calmatpro.CalMatPro.cp0
documentation:
Inactive specific heat.

Gives the specific heat for a given temperature

methods.name: heatrapy.mats.calmatpro.CalMatPro.k0
documentation:
Inactive thermal conductivity.

Gives the thermal conductivity for a given temperature

methods.name: heatrapy.mats.calmatpro.CalMatPro.ka
documentation:
Active thermal conductivity.

Gives the active thermal conductivity for a given temperature

methods.name: heatrapy.mats.calmatpro.CalMatPro.rho0
documentation:
Inactive density.

Gives the density for a given temperature

methods.name: heatrapy.mats.calmatpro.CalMatPro.rhoa
documentation:
Active density.

Gives the active density for a given temperature

methods.name: heatrapy.mats.calmatpro.CalMatPro.lheat0
documentation:
Inactive latent heat.

Gives the latent heat list

methods.name: heatrapy.mats.calmatpro.CalMatPro.lheata
documentation:
Active latent heat.

Gives the active latent heat list

CalMatPro class.

Loads and gives physical properties of materials from the database

modules.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects
documentation:
objects.

This submodule includes the unidimensional object classes.

modules.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects.system
documentation:
Contains the class system_objects.

Used to compute systems of thermal objects.

classes.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects.system.SystemObjects

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects.system.SystemObjects.__init__
documentation:
System object initialization.

`number_objects` is the integer number of thermal objects. `materials`
is the list of strings of all the used materials present in
`material_path`. `amb_temperature` is the ambient temperature of the
whole system. `object_length` is the list of thermal object lengths
(spacial steps). `dx` and `dt` are the space and time steps,
respectively. `file_name` is the file name where the temperature is
saved. `boundaries` is a list of tuples of length two that define each
boundary condition for temperature. If 0 the boundary condition is
insulation. `materials_path` is absolute path of the materials
database. If false, then the materials database is the standard
heatrapy database.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects.system.SystemObjects.contact_filter
documentation:
Filter self.contacts by thermal object id.

object: thermal object id

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects.system.SystemObjects.contact_add
documentation:
Add contact to self.contacts.

The `contact` parameter is a tuple of length 3 (one element for thermal
object A, one for thermal object B, and one for the heat transfer
coefficient). Each thermal object element is a tuple of length 2 where
the first element is the index of the thermal object and the second is
the spatial point index.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects.system.SystemObjects.contact_remove
documentation:
Contact removal.

Removes all contacts between `object_one` id and `object_two` id.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects.system.SystemObjects.change_boundaries
documentation:
Change boundaries.

Changes the `boundaries` of `object_id`.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects.system.SystemObjects.compute
documentation:
Compute the thermal process.

Computes the system for `time_interval`, and writes into the
`file_name` file every `write_interval` time steps. Four different
solvers can be used: `'explicit_general'`, `'explicit_k(x)'`,
`'implicit_general'`, and `'implicit_k(x)'`. If `verbose = True`, then
the progress of the computation is shown.

System_objects class.

This class creates a system of unidimensional thermal objects, establishes
contact between them and computes the respective thermal processes.

modules.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects.object
documentation:
Contains the class object.

Used to create unidimensional thermal objects and apply some methods. It does
not include the compute method.

classes.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects.object.Object

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects.object.Object.__init__
documentation:
Thermal object initialization.

`amb_temperature` is the ambient temperature of the whole system.
`materials` is the list of strings of all the used materials present in
`material_path`. `borders` is a list of the points where there is a
change of material. `materials_order` is a list of the materials list
indexes that defines the material properties given by borders. `dx` and
`dt` are the space and time steps, respectively. `file_name` is the
file name where the temperature is saved. `boundaries` is a list of two
entries that define the boundary condition for temperature. If 0 the
boundary condition is insulation. `initial_state` is the initial state
of the materials. True if there are an applied field and False if them
field is absent. `materials_path` is absolute path of the materials
database. If false, then the materials database is the standard
heatrapy database. `Q0` is a list of fixed heat source coefficient and
`Q` is a list of temperature dependent heat source coefficient.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects.object.Object.activate
documentation:
Activation of the material.

activates a given space interval of the material, between the
`initial_point` and `final_point`.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects.object.Object.deactivate
documentation:
Deactivation of the material.

deactivates a given space interval of the material, between the
`initial_point` and `final_point`.

Object class.

This class creates a unidimensional thermal object. It includes two
methods to apply and remove fields.

Used to compute one time step of 2D systems with fixed thermal
conductivity.

modules.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.solvers

Solvers.

This submodule contains the solvers for the computation of the two-dimensional
thermal processes.

modules.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects.single
documentation:
Contains the class single_object.

Used to compute single thermal objects.

classes.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects.single.SingleObject

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects.single.SingleObject.__init__
documentation:
Thermal object initialization.

`amb_temperature` is the ambient temperature of the whole system.
`materials` is the list of strings of all the used materials present in
`material_path`. `borders` is a list of the points where there is a
change of material. `materials_order` is a list of the materials list
indexes that defines the material properties given by borders. `dx` and
`dt` are the space and time steps, respectively. `file_name` is the
file name where the temperature is saved. `boundaries` is a list of two
entries that define the boundary condition for temperature. If 0 the
boundary condition is insulation. `initial_state` is the initial state
of the materials. True if there are an applied field and False if them
field is absent. `materials_path` is absolute path of the materials
database. If false, then the materials database is the standard
heatrapy database. `draw` is a list of strings representing the online
plots. In this version only `'temperature'` can be potted. If the list
is empty, then no drawing is performed. `draw_scale` is a list of two
values, representing the minimum and maximum temperature to be drawn.
If None, there are no limits.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects.single.SingleObject.show_figure
documentation:
Plotting.

Initializes a specific live plotting. `figure_type` is a string
identifying the plotting. This version only allows the plotting of the
'temperature'. `draw_scale` defines the range of temperatures. If None,
this range is found automatically for every frame.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects.single.SingleObject.activate
documentation:
Activation.

Activates the thermal object between `initial_point` to `final_point`.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects.single.SingleObject.deactivate
documentation:
Deactivation.

Deactivates the thermal object between `initial_point` to
`final_point`.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects.single.SingleObject.change_power
documentation:
Heat power source change.

Changes the coeficients for the heat power sources by a value of power
from `initial_point` to `final_point`. `power_type` is a string that
represents the type of coefficient, i.e. 'Q' or 'Q0'.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects.single.SingleObject.change_boundaries
documentation:
Boundary change.

Changes the `boundaries` variable.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_1.objects.single.SingleObject.compute
documentation:
Compute the thermal process.

Computes the system for time_interval seconds, and writes into the
`file_name` file every `write_interval` time steps. Four different
solvers can be used: `'explicit_general'`, `'explicit_k(x)'`,
`'implicit_general'`, and `'implicit_k(x)'`. If `verbose = True`, then
the progress of the computation progress is shown.

Single_object class.

This class solves numerically the heat conduction equation for 1 dimension
of a single material(s). The class has 6 methods.

modules.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects
documentation:
objects.

This submodule includes the two-dimensional object classes.

modules.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.solvers.explicit_k
documentation:
Contains the 2D explicit_k solver.

Used to compute two-dimensional thermal processes

functions.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.solvers.explicit_k.explicit_k
documentation:
explicit_k solver.

Used to compute one time step of 2D systems with position dependent thermal
conductivities.

modules.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.system
documentation:
Contains the classes system_objects and single_object.

Used to compute two-dimensional thermal objects

classes.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.system.SystemObjects

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.system.SystemObjects.__init__
documentation:
System object initialization.

`number_objects` is the integer number of thermal objects. `materials`
is the list of strings of all the used materials present in
`material_path`. `amb_temperature` is the ambient temperature of the
whole system. `object_length` is the list of thermal object lengths
(tuple of spacial steps). `dx` and `dy` are the space steps along the
x- and y-axis, respectively. dt is the time step. `file_name` is the
file name where the temperature is saved. `boundaries` is a list of
four entries that define the boundary condition for temperature (left,
right, bottom, top). If 0, the boundary condition is insulation.
`materials_path` is absolute path of the materials database. If false,
then the materials database is the standard heatrapy database.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.system.SystemObjects.contact_filter
documentation:
Filter thermal contacts.

Filter thermal contacts by `object_id`.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.system.SystemObjects.contact_add
documentation:
Add contact to self.contacts.

`contact` is a thermal contact tuple of length 3, where the first and
second entries correspond to tuples of the thermal objects and points
(object_id, (x,y)), and the third entry is the heat transfer
coefficient.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.system.SystemObjects.contact_remove
documentation:
Contact removal.

Removes all contacts between `object_one` id and `object_two` id.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.system.SystemObjects.change_boundaries
documentation:
Change boundaries.

Changes the `boundaries` variable of `object_id`.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.system.SystemObjects.compute
documentation:
Compute the thermal process.

Computes the system for `time_interval`, and writes into the
`file_name` file every `write_interval` time steps. Two different
solvers can be used: `'explicit_general'` and `'explicit_k(x)'`. If
verbose = True, then the progress of the computation is shown.

System_objects class.

This class creates a system of 2D thermal objects, establishes contact
between them and computes the respective thermal processes.

modules.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.single
documentation:
Contains the class single_object.

Used to compute single two-dimensional system models.

classes.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.single.SingleObject

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.single.SingleObject.__init__
documentation:
Object initialization.

`amb_temperature` is the ambient temperature of the whole system.
`materials` is the background material present in `material_path`.
`dx`, `dy` are the space steps along the x- and y-axis, respectively.
`dt` is the time step. `file_name` is the file name where the
temperature is saved. `boundaries` is a list of four entries that
define the boundary condition for temperature (left, right, bottom,
top). If 0 the boundary condition is insulation. `initial_state` is the
initial state of the materials. True if there are an applied field and
False if them field is absent. `materials_path` is absolute path of the
materials database. If false, then the materials database is the
standard heatrapy database. `draw` is a list of strings representing
the online plots. In this version live plotting can be performed for
`temperature`, `materials`, `state`, `Q` and `Q0`. If the list is
empty, then no drawing is performed. `draw_scale` is a list of two
values, representing the minimum and maximum temperature to be drawn.
If None, there are no limits. `Q0` is a list of fixed heat source
coefficient and `Q` is a list of temperature dependent heat source
coefficient.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.single.SingleObject.show_figure
documentation:
Plotting.

Initializes a specific plotting. `figure_type` is a string identifying
the plotting. In this version live plotting can be performed for
`temperature`, `materials`, `state`, `Q` and `Q0`.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.single.SingleObject.activate
documentation:
Activation of the material.

Activates a given piece of material. If `shape` is `'square'`, then the
`initial_point` is the tuple (x,y) of the bottom left point and the
`final_point` is the tuple (x,y) of the top right point. If the shape
is `'circle'`, the `initial_point` is the tuple (x,y) of the center of
the circle and `final_point` is its radius.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.single.SingleObject.deactivate
documentation:
Deactivation of the material.

Deactivates a given piece of material. If `shape` is `'square'`, then
the `initial_point` is the tuple (x,y) of the bottom left point and the
`final_point` is the tuple (x,y) of the top right point. If the shape
is `'circle'`, the `initial_point` is the tuple (x,y) of the center of
the circle and `final_point` is its radius.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.single.SingleObject.change_boundaries
documentation:
Boundary change.

Changes the `boundaries` variable.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.single.SingleObject.change_material
documentation:
Material change.

Changes the material of a given piece of the background material. If
`shape` is `'square'`, then the initial_point is the tuple (x,y) of the
bottom left point and the `length` is the tuple (x,y) of the length. If
the shape is `'circle'`, the `initial_point` is the tuple (x,y) of the
center of the circle and `length` is its radius.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.single.SingleObject.change_power
documentation:
Power change.

Changes the power matrix of the thermal object. If `shape` is
`'square'`, then the `initial_point` is the tuple (x,y) of the bottom
left point and the `final_point` is the tuple (x,y) of the top right
point. If the `shape` is `'circle'`, the `initial_point` is the tuple
(x,y) of the center of the circle and `final_point` is its radius.
`power` is the value of the power to add, and `power_type` is the type
of power to be introduced, which has the value `'Q'` if it is
temperature dependent and `'Q0'` if it is temperature independent.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.single.SingleObject.compute
documentation:
Compute the thermal process.

Computes the system for `time_interval`, and writes into the
`file_name` file every `write_interval` time steps. Two different
solvers can be used: `'explicit_general'` and `'explicit_k(x)'`. If
verbose = True, then the progress of the computation is shown.

Single_object class.

This class solves numerically the two-dimensional heat conduction equation.

modules.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.object
documentation:
Contains the class object.

Used to create two-dimensional thermal objects, and apply some methods. It does
not include the compute method.

classes.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.object.Object

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.object.Object.__init__
documentation:
Thermal object initialization.

`amb_temperature` is the ambient temperature of the whole system.
`materials` is the background material present in `material_path`.
`dx`, `dy` are the space steps along the x- and y-axis, respectively.
`dt` is the time step. `file_name` is the file name where the
temperature is saved. `boundaries` is a list of four entries that
define the boundary condition for temperature (left, right, bottom,
top). If 0 the boundary condition is insulation. `initial_state` is the
initial state of the materials. True if there are an applied field and
False if them field is absent. `materials_path` is absolute path of the
materials database. If false, then the materials database is the
standard heatrapy database. `Q0` is a list of fixed heat source
coefficient and `Q` is a list of temperature dependent heat source
coefficient.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.object.Object.activate
documentation:
Activation of the material.

Activates a given piece of material. If `shape` is `'square'`, then the
`initial_point` is the tuple (x,y) of the bottom left point and the
`final_point` is the tuple (x,y) of the top right point. If the shape
is `'circle'`, the `initial_point` is the tuple (x,y) of the center of
the circle and `final_point` is its radius.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.object.Object.deactivate
documentation:
Deactivation of the material.

Deactivates a given piece of material. If `shape` is `'square'`, then
the `initial_point` is the tuple (x,y) of the bottom left point and the
`final_point` is the tuple (x,y) of the top right point. If the shape
is `'circle'`, the `initial_point` is the tuple (x,y) of the center of
the circle and `final_point` is its radius.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.object.Object.square
documentation:
Material adding with rectangle shape.

Adds a new material with a rectangle shape, where `initial_point` is
the bottom left (x,y) tuple, `length` is the length along the two axis,
state is the initial state of the material and `materials_path` is the
absolute path of the materials database.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.object.Object.circle
documentation:
Material adding with circle shape.

Adds a new material with a circle shape, where `initial_point` is the
(x,y) tuple of the center of the circle, `radius` is the radius of the
circle, state is the initial state of the material and `materials_path`
is the absolute path of the materials database.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.object.Object.power_add
documentation:
Power adding.

Adds a power matrix to the thermal object. If `shape` is `'square'`,
then the `initial_point` is the tuple (x,y) of the bottom left point
and the `final_point` is the tuple (x,y) of the top right point. If the
`shape` is `'circle'`, the `initial_point` is the tuple (x,y) of the
center of the circle and `final_point` is its radius. `power` is the
value of the power to add, and `power_type` is the type of power to be
introduced, which has the value `'Q'` if it is temperature dependent
and `'Q0'` if it is temperature independent.

methods.name: heatrapy.dimension_2.objects.object.Object.power_reset
documentation:
Power reset.

Resets the power matrix with `power_type` `'Q'` or `'Q0'`, which
corresponds to the power temperature dependent and temperature
independent, respectively.

Object class.

This class creates a two-dimensional thermal object. It includes two
methods to apply and remove fields.
